title: BS in Computer Science and Game Development - Khoury College of Computer Sciences
text:
BS in Computer Science and Game Development - Khoury College of Computer Sciences
BS in Computer Science and Game Development
Apply now
Request info
The BS in Computer Science and Game Development combined major (Khoury College and the College of Arts, Media and Design) gives you the foundational background you need to succeed in the highly competitive game industry. You will build and develop games and playable media experiences while completing courses in computer science, specialized game technology, and design. Through interdisciplinary courses and hands-on learning, you will develop your creative and entrepreneurial abilities as well as create a strong portfolio of game pieces.
Explore program requirements in the course catalog
Program details
Career prospects
Career prospects
A combined major in computer science and game development can lead to exciting career opportunities in the dynamic and rapidly growing gaming industry. Some possible career paths include:
Game developer
Game designer
Quality assurance tester
Virtual reality (VR) or augmented reality (AR) developer
Game producer
Co-ops and internships
Co-ops and internships
During your co-ops you will work in industry settings, applying your skills and building important connections. Our students’ recent co-op experiences include:
Engineering intern at Disruptor Beam
Engineering intern at Demiurge Studios
Global training software developer co-op at Bose Corporation
Learn more about co-ops
Research opportunities
Research opportunities
Work with leading researchers in state-of-the art facilities on cutting-edge research projects at the forefront of gaming. Some of our research areas include:
Games
Machine learning
Human-computer interaction
Explore research at Khoury College
Learning outcomes
Learning outcomes
In this program, you’ll learn to:
Apply design principles in the construction of software systems of varying complexity.
Use current techniques, skills, and tools necessary for effective and secure computing practice.
Apply mathematical foundations, algorithmic principles, and computer science theory in the modeling and design of computer-based systems in a way that demonstrates comprehension of the tradeoffs involved in design choices.
Campus location
Boston
Located in the heart of Boston, Northeastern’s oldest campus is home to a vibrant community of students, researchers, and faculty working across disciplines to build a better world through CS.
Explore Boston's campus
Applications and admissions
Deadlines
Deadlines
Learn more about our undergraduate early action, early decision, and regular decision options.
Explore deadlines
Degree costs
Degree costs
Pursuing a world-class education is an investment in your future. It’s also one of the most important decisions you’ll ever make. Visit our Undergraduate Admissions site to find current Northeastern University costs. You can also estimate the cost of your degree using our College Cost Estimator or Net Price Calculator.
Find and estimate degree costs
Financial aid and scholarships
Financial aid and scholarships
Northeastern University offers a range of financial aid and scholarship opportunities to help you fund your education. You’ll receive an initial financial aid package that outlines your options after admission. Once you enroll, Khoury College has multiple scholarship opportunities for current students to continue supporting your studies. We also encourage all students to submit the FAFSA to determine your eligibility for federal aid and loans.
Learn more about scholarship opportunities
Have questions?
Have questions?
Let us know if you have any questions about the application or enrollment process. We are here to help!
Contact an enrollment counselor
Meet our faculty
Get to know the engaged, experienced professors who are here to support you every step of the way.
View all faculty
Caglar Yildirim
Caglar Yildirim is an associate teaching professor at Khoury College. His research combines human–computer interaction, mixed reality, data visualization, games, and applied machine learning.
Read bio

The BS in Computer Science and Game Development program is an interdisciplinary collaboration with the College of Arts, Media and Design.
